api/v3/handlers/customers/billing.purpose: v3 HTTP handlers for customer-scoped billing operations: reading and writing the customer billing profile override, app-specific customer data (Stripe, CustomInvoicing, Sandbox), and Stripe session management (checkout + portal). Primary constraint: all mutations must guard against deleted customers before calling domain services.

patterns.name: HandlerWithArgs factory per operation
patterns.description: Each exported method on handler returns a typed httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[Request, Response, Params]. The decoder resolves namespace, validates the customer is not deleted, then builds the domain input. Error encoders are appended via httptransport.AppendOptions.
patterns.example: func (h *handler) GetCustomerBilling() GetCustomerBillingHandler { return httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decoderFn, operationFn, commonhttp.JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus[...](http.StatusOK), httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, ...)...) }

patterns.name: Deleted-customer guard in decoder
patterns.description: Every mutation decoder calls h.customerService.GetCustomer and checks cus.IsDeleted(), returning apierrors.NewGoneError if true. Skip this and deleted-customer writes go undetected.
patterns.example: if cus.IsDeleted() { return ..., apierrors.NewGoneError(ctx, errors.New("customer is deleted")) }

patterns.name: App-type switch for customer data
patterns.description: Handlers that read or write app-specific data switch on application.GetType() over app.AppTypeStripe / AppTypeCustomInvoicing / AppTypeSandbox and return apierrors.NewInternalError for unknown types. Keep get_billing.go and update_billing.go in sync when adding a new app type.
patterns.example: switch application.GetType() { case app.AppTypeStripe: ... case app.AppTypeCustomInvoicing: ... case app.AppTypeSandbox: ... default: return resp, apierrors.NewInternalError(ctx, fmt.Errorf("unsupported app type")) }

patterns.name: Goverter for v3-to-legacy enum conversions
patterns.description: convert.go declares goverter variable-set functions (FromAPI*/ToAPI*) with enum mapping annotations; convert.gen.go is generated. Never hand-edit convert.gen.go. Add new enum mappings to convert.go and run go generate.

patterns.name: Domain-specific error encoder chained after generic
patterns.description: error_encoder.go matches billing.NotFoundError->404, billing.ValidationError->400, billing.UpdateAfterDeleteError->409, billing.AppError->400. It is always appended AFTER apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder() in httptransport.AppendOptions.

patterns.name: Stripe app resolved via billing profile
patterns.description: Checkout session creation calls h.billingService.ResolveStripeAppIDFromBillingProfile to get the AppID; portal session creation uses h.billingService.GetCustomerApp with AppType filter. Do not hardcode app IDs.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Handler interface and the handler struct with its constructor New(). The handler holds billingService, customerService, stripeService. Note: appService is declared as a struct field but NOT set in New() — do not add it to New's signature without updating all call sites.
key_file_guides.watch_for: handler.appService field is declared but not set in New(); passing app.Service to New() will break existing call sites.

key_file_guides.role: Source of truth for all v3-to-legacy and domain type conversions; goverter annotations drive convert.gen.go generation. Run go generate after any change.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new enum variant in TypeSpec without a matching goverter:enum:map annotation causes convert.gen.go to error on the unknown variant at runtime.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by goverter from convert.go. DO NOT EDIT.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any manual edit is overwritten on next go generate.

key_file_guides.role: Package-local error encoder matching billing-specific error types to HTTP codes. Must be appended AFTER GenericErrorEncoder so generic errors are caught first.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Reversing the encoder order causes billing.ValidationError to fall through to 500 instead of 400.

key_file_guides.role: Upserts both the billing profile override and app customer data atomically. If only app data should change, use update_billing_app_data.go instead.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Both files share identical app-type switch logic; keep them in sync when adding a new app type.

anti_patterns: Calling billing domain services without first checking customer.IsDeleted() in the decoder
Hand-editing convert.gen.go instead of updating convert.go annotations and regenerating
Hardcoding a Stripe app ID instead of resolving via billingService.ResolveStripeAppIDFromBillingProfile
Adding app-type handling in only one of get_billing.go or update_billing.go

decisions.decision: Stripe-specific session endpoints (checkout, portal) live in this package alongside generic billing profile endpoints
decisions.rationale: All endpoints share the same customer-scoped path prefix and require identical deleted-customer validation; collocating them avoids duplicated validation logic.

decisions.decision: Goverter is used for v3-to-legacy enum conversions rather than hand-written switch statements
decisions.rationale: The enum space is large (10+ enums for Stripe checkout options); goverter with @error on unknown values gives compile-time safety and avoids manual mapping drift.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new customer billing GET endpoint following the established pattern

api/v3/handlers/customers/charges.purpose: v3 HTTP handler for listing a customer's billing charges (flat-fee and usage-based) in the v3 API. Primary constraint: credit-purchase charges are intentionally excluded at the query layer and must never appear in this API surface.

patterns.name: HandlerWithArgs with inline pagination validation
patterns.description: ListCustomerCharges decoder builds a pagination.Page from query params with explicit page.Validate() that returns apierrors.NewBadRequestError on invalid page. Default page is (1, 20).

patterns.name: Credit-purchase exclusion via ChargeTypes filter
patterns.description: The request always sets ChargeTypes: []meta.ChargeType{meta.ChargeTypeFlatFee, meta.ChargeTypeUsageBased}. convertChargeToAPI returns an explicit error if ChargeTypeCreditPurchase ever reaches it as a defensive measure.

patterns.name: Realizations always expanded for booked totals
patterns.description: expands always includes meta.ExpandRealizations because booked totals require realization run data. RealtimeUsage is only added when the caller requests BillingChargesExpandRealTimeUsage.
patterns.example:
expands := meta.Expands{meta.ExpandRealizations}
if slices.Contains(*args.Params.Expand, api.BillingChargesExpandRealTimeUsage) { expands = expands.With(meta.ExpandRealtimeUsage) }

patterns.name: Sort field whitelist validation
patterns.description: Acceptable sort fields are "id", "created_at", "service_period.from", "billing_period.from". validChargesSortField rejects anything else with a 400 listing allowed values.
patterns.example: if !validChargesSortField(sort.Field) { return ..., apierrors.NewBadRequestError(ctx, fmt.Errorf("unsupported sort field: %s"), ...) }

patterns.name: Union-type dispatch in convertChargeToAPI
patterns.description: convertChargeToAPI switches on charge.Type(), calls AsFlatFeeCharge or AsUsageBasedCharge, maps to the API struct, then calls out.FromBillingFlatFeeCharge or out.FromBillingUsageBasedCharge. Both the domain-to-struct and struct-to-union steps must succeed and errors must be checked.
patterns.example: if err := out.FromBillingFlatFeeCharge(apiFF); err != nil { return out, fmt.Errorf("setting flat fee charge union: %w", err) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Handler interface and handler struct. Constructor New() takes resolveNamespace + billingcharges.ChargeService. No billing.Service dependency — only the charges sub-service.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding handler methods that need billing.Service will require extending the struct and constructor.

key_file_guides.role: Only handler in this package. Decodes pagination, sort, status filter, and expand from query params; calls service.ListCharges; maps results via convertChargeToAPI.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Status filter parsing uses a type-safe switch in parseChargeStatusFilterSlice — add new status values there when meta.ChargeStatus gains new variants.

key_file_guides.role: All domain-to-API mapping functions. Many are exported (ConvertClosedPeriodToAPI, ConvertCurrencyCodeToAPI, ConvertChargeStatusToAPI, etc.) and reused by sibling handler packages (e.g., customerscredits).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Exported converters are shared; changing their signatures affects callers in sibling packages. DynamicPrice and PackagePrice types are not supported in toAPIBillingPrice and return an error.

anti_patterns: Including meta.ChargeTypeCreditPurchase in the ChargeTypes filter (credit purchases belong to the credits API)
Omitting meta.ExpandRealizations from expands (breaks booked totals computation)
Using a generic string sort field without validating against the whitelist in validChargesSortField
Hand-writing the union set call (out.FromBillingFlatFeeCharge) without checking the error it returns

decisions.decision: Credit-purchase charges are excluded from this endpoint and served by the credits API
decisions.rationale: Credit purchases have a distinct lifecycle (settlement, ledger funding) that belongs to the credits domain; mixing them into the charges list would conflate two different billing concepts.

decisions.decision: Realization runs are always fetched even when not requested by the caller
decisions.rationale: Booked totals are computed from persisted realization runs; returning a charge without them would produce zero totals, which is incorrect for active charges.

code_examples.scenario: Convert a domain Charge to the API union type with proper error handling

api/v3/handlers/customers/credits.purpose: v3 HTTP handlers for customer credit-grant lifecycle and balance queries: create/get/list grants, update external settlement status, get credit balance, and list ledger transactions. Primary constraint: this package is gated by credits.enabled; the handler is wired to noop ledger/balance services when credits are off.

patterns.name: Local customerBalanceFacade interface
patterns.description: handler.go defines a narrow customerBalanceFacade interface (GetBalance, GetBalances, ListCreditTransactions) rather than importing the full customerbalance service. This keeps the dependency surface explicit and testable.

patterns.name: Settlement-type dispatch for purchase block
patterns.description: toAPICreditGrantPurchase switches on settlement.Type() (invoice, external, promotional). Promotional grants return nil purchase. Unknown types return an error. The outer toAPIBillingCreditGrant handles the nil case gracefully.

patterns.name: Base64-JSON cursor for transaction pagination
patterns.description: encodeBillingCreditTransactionCursor/decodeBillingCreditTransactionCursor marshal a {booked_at, created_at, id} struct to JSON then base64. Cursors are validated after decoding via ledgerCursor.Validate().

patterns.name: models.ValidationIssue for domain-specific errors without a custom errorEncoder
patterns.description: errors.go uses models.NewValidationIssue with a typed ErrorCode constant, WithCriticalSeverity, commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute(400), and WithFieldString. This lets apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder handle it without a package-local error encoder.

patterns.name: Customer existence validation before balance query
patterns.description: get_balance.go calls h.customerService.GetCustomer first to validate existence before querying balanceFacade.GetBalances. This ensures a 404 is returned for unknown customers before attempting a balance lookup.
patterns.example:
_, err := h.customerService.GetCustomer(ctx, customer.GetCustomerInput{CustomerID: &request.CustomerID})
if err != nil { return GetCustomerCreditBalanceResponse{}, err }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Handler interface (6 methods) and handler struct with ledger, accountResolver, balanceFacade, creditGrantService. New() wires all of them.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ledger and accountResolver are wired to noops by app/common when credits.enabled=false; handlers must not assume they are real implementations.

key_file_guides.role: All domain-to-API and API-to-domain mapping. toAPIBillingCreditGrant is the primary domain-to-API converter; fromAPICreateCreditGrantRequest is the primary API-to-domain converter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: fromAPICreateCreditGrantRequest returns an error for feature filters (not yet supported); keep the TODO and do not silently ignore the filter.

key_file_guides.role: Defines newCreditGrantExternalSettlementStatusInvalid using models.NewValidationIssue with HTTP status attribute. This package intentionally has no custom errorEncoder() — unlike sibling packages.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add an errorEncoder() function here; the ValidationIssue+WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute pattern is the intended mechanism.

key_file_guides.role: Handler for PATCH external settlement status transitions. Only authorized and settled are valid target statuses; pending is explicitly rejected in fromAPIBillingCreditPurchasePaymentSettlementStatus.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The 'pending' status must remain rejected; a grant cannot be downgraded to pending once advanced.

anti_patterns: Assuming ledger or accountResolver are real implementations — they may be noops when credits.enabled=false
Adding a package-local errorEncoder() — this package relies on models.ValidationIssue + WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute for error encoding
Allowing the 'pending' settlement status to succeed in fromAPIBillingCreditPurchasePaymentSettlementStatus
Returning a non-nil purchase block for SettlementTypePromotional grants
Silently ignoring the feature filter in fromAPICreateCreditGrantRequest instead of returning an error

decisions.decision: customerBalanceFacade is a local interface rather than importing the full customerbalance.Service
decisions.rationale: Narrows the dependency surface to only the three methods this handler needs, making tests easier to mock and preventing accidental use of write operations.

decisions.decision: ValidationIssue with WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute replaces a custom errorEncoder for domain validation errors
decisions.rationale: apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder already inspects the HTTP status attribute on ValidationIssues; adding a second encoder would be redundant and could cause double-handling.

code_examples.scenario: Create a domain-specific validation error usable by GenericErrorEncoder without a custom error encoder
